projectDescription: Iris Code is a VS Code extension and CLI tool that scores code health on every save, surfaces problems in the editor, and enforces quality thresholds via git hooks, build hooks, and CI pipelines — all running entirely on your machine with no code upload.
keyFeatures: Per-file health score (0–100) recomputed on every save
Workspace and folder-level aggregate analysis with ranked hotspots
Gate Preview — read-only enforcement posture check against 5 preset thresholds
Git pre-push hook enforcement blocking pushes below your health threshold
Build hook enforcement for Node, Go, and Python projects
GitHub Actions CI integration with inline PR annotations
Hardcoded secrets detection (two-layer: variable names + credential formats)
Inline diagnostics as editor squiggles and VS Code Problems panel entries
Dependency audit for outdated versions and CVEs (npm, Go modules, Python)
Full CLI suite (`@iris-code/cli`) for terminal and CI use without VS Code
Config presets (legacy, balanced, typescript, strict, security) shareable via `.irisconfig.json`
Config Studio — visual editor with live JSON preview and one-click sync to VS Code
Custom health score weights (Pro) to tune how findings affect the 0–100 score
Trend tracking — daily health score snapshots showing codebase improvement or regression
Supports TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, and Python
publicApiSurface: .irisconfig.json: minHealthScore, presetId, functionLengthThreshold, fileLengthThreshold, gateMaxSecrets, gateMaxComplexity, gateMaxFileLength, gateMaxSmellsPerFile, healthScoreWeights, severityOverrides, ignoreFiles, inlineDiagnostics
IRIS_LICENCE_TOKEN environment variable for CI authentication
